steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures
steps.content: Left of blank: preposition “of” expecting a noun phrase as its object. Right of blank: noun “protagonist” followed by a comma and appositive. The blank contains a noun phrase with modifiers and a possessive form.

steps.title: Apply number agreement
steps.content:
Agreement in number: possessives and nouns must match the singular or plural nature of what they refer to.

The singular novel requires “the novel's” and the plural poems require “poems,” so the correct phrase is “poems putatively written by the novel's.”

The phrases “poem's putatively written by the novel's” and “poem's putatively written by the novels'” incorrectly make poems singular possessive; “poem's putatively written by the novels'” and “poems putatively written by the novels'” incorrectly make novel plural possessive.

That confirms A is correct.
